changes.rationale: New nullable string column at consumable (renamed passthrough from base.endowment_value_flag). Pure additive surface change. CDE flag set on a newly-introduced column (not a CDE flag change on an existing contract column, which would have triggered MAJOR).

Deliberate row-count drop of ~25-40 rows. Three v1.3 contract assertions become false: contract YAML record_count: 2675, row_count_guarantee: 2675, and CON-IFP-001 strict equality with base. Spec correctly schedules contract amendment (§6, §8) and CON-IFP-001 split (§2 Decision E) into 001a (upper bound, P0) + 001b (lower bound, P1). Below MAJOR threshold because no fork (v1/v2 coexistence) is required — the named primary downstream consumer reads base, not consumable.
Pure additive DQ rule mirroring RAW-IPF-013 at the consumer surface. No schema change, no row-count change, no transformer change.
New non-null date column at consumable (restored passthrough from base). Pure additive surface change. NOT NULL preserves the upstream base guarantee.

evidence: EADA spec §5 Sources line 247: raw.eada LEFT JOIN base.ipeds_finance on UNITID
EADA spec §6 Sources line 336: base.ipeds_finance FULL OUTER JOIN base.eada on UNITID
consumable-institution-aura.json CON-AUR-001 SQL computes bound from (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.ipeds_finance), not consumable
named_primary_consumer.impact: ZERO — base zone is pure-additive under v1.4 (one column passthrough). Base row count unchanged at 2,675. CON-AUR-001 row-count tolerance band [2675, 4715] continues to hold.

other_consumers_swept.other_gold_tables: 0 cross-table joins target this consumable; CON-IFP-008/008b reference consumable.career_outcomes which carries no system-office UNITIDs

hardcoded_2675_assertions.this_table_contract: WILL BREAK without amendment — spec §6 / §8 schedules update
hardcoded_2675_assertions.this_table_dq_rule_CON_IFP_001: WILL BREAK without split — spec §2 Decision E schedules split
hardcoded_2675_assertions.institution_aura_contract: NO BREAK — references base.ipeds_finance row count, not consumable
hardcoded_2675_assertions.career_outcomes_overlap_CON_IFP_008: NO BREAK — system-office UNITIDs are not in career_outcomes; numerator + denominator + ratio unchanged
hardcoded_2675_assertions.golden_datasets: 0 hits — system-office UNITIDs are not anchor schools

blast_radius.fork_rationale: MEDIUM, not MAJOR. Forking (v1/v2 coexistence) would be over-engineering. The named primary downstream consumer reads base, not consumable. The contract amendment + CON-IFP-001 split is sufficient.

decision_rationale: All four item classifications match the spec's §7 expected severity table. Overall MEDIUM is below MAJOR threshold; no fork required. Row-count contract change is contained to this table's own YAML and own CON-IFP-001 rule (both scheduled for amendment in §6/§8/Decision E). Zero third-party consumer breaks. Standing user constraints all PASS. Chaos scoped to 5 new rules + filter inverse-misclassification scenario.
conditions: Contract amendment to governance/data-contracts/consumable-ipeds-finance-profile.yaml per §6 Data Contract delta table (record_count, row_count_guarantee, row_count_tolerance_note, new column entries, cde_columns_list update)
CON-IFP-001 split in governance/dq-rules/consumable-ipeds-finance-profile.json per §2 Decision E (001a upper bound P0; 001b lower bound P1)

Chaos pass must include the inverse-misclassification scenario: a real teaching institution with 'Office' or 'System' in legal name plus instruction expenses >= $1M must survive the filter (§2 Decision B AND-clause empirical confirmation)
